curation_notes: Locations derived from the patch diff. The core vulnerability is in authority/authorize.go UseToken, which silently ignored all errors from GetTokenID — meaning ACME and SCEP provisioners (which always return errors from GetTokenID because they don't use token-based flows) had their entire token reuse protection skipped. The ACME and SCEP GetTokenID methods returned generic errors instead of typed sentinel errors, contributing to the silent bypass. Additionally, authority/tls.go Revoke was missing a check that the serial number in the token matches the serial in the request. The patch is noisy (15 non-test source files changed) due to additional hardening across multiple provisioners, error handling improvements, and the introduction of new sentinel error types. Pre-assigned class auth-bypass from CWE-306 is correct — the vulnerability allows bypassing authentication/authorization checks for ACME and SCEP provisioners.
